channel.title: Babylon Burning
channel.description: Babylon Burning specializes in reggae music, new and old - from the rocksteady of the late 60s, through the roots and rockers era of the 70s, to the foundation dancehall sounds of the early 80s, right up to the freshest releases from Jamaica and around the world.

item.title: never stoop down
item.description: Starting with the usual vintage classics, before showcasing some new music with a vintage sound, and then revisiting some late 90s and early 2000s riddims. Plus the brand new Roots Rock Reggae riddim, and a new killer from Kartel.
item.author: Jesse I

item.title: what kind of world
item.description: Featuring the new riddims Africa Movement from Conquering Lion Records and Hi and Low from Ajang Music... revisiting the late 90s riddims Persistence and Babatunde... new albums from Xana Romeo and Shanti Yalah... version excursions on the What Kind of World and Far East riddims... and the usual vintage classics.
item.author: Jesse I

item.title: RIP Stephen "Cat" Coore
item.description: Paying respects to the great guitarist, cellist, singer, songwriter and founding member of Third World, Stephen "Cat" Coore (1956-2026). Plus new riddims Manuka Honey from Giddimani Music & Sinky Beatz, and Heart Beauty from Riva Nile, and great new music from Protoje & Damian Marley, Xana Romeo, Irie Love, Shenseea, Haile Celestial, Dactah Chando, Benjammin and more.
item.author: Jesse I

item.title: RIP Lowell Fillmore "Sly" Dunbar
item.description: Paying respects to the legendary Jamaican drummer and producer Lowell Fillmore “Sly” Dunbar (1952-2026) with two hours of songs he played on, produced, or both.
item.author: Jesse I

item.title: bad road
item.description: After a false start due to a fire alarm at PBS, some classic 70s roots reggae, and then a very special live studio interview with Jamaican artist Norris Man.
item.author: Jesse I

item.title: undying love
item.description: Featuring some sweet lovers tunes, new and old... revisiting the Automatic riddim on Penthouse and Love Potion riddim on Don Corleon... and new music from Marcus Gad, Perfect Giddimani, and Keznamdi...
item.author: Jesse I

item.title: keep it rootikal
item.description: Featuring riddims Rootikal from Koastal Kings and Film Stone from Countree Hype... new tunes from Runkus, Roe Summerz, Marcus I, Young Kulcha, Ras-I & Kabaka Pyramid... new Melbourne-produced roots from Svaha Records and Jamaica's Alovera... some early 80s foundation dancehall from George Phang's Power House label... and the usual 70s reggae classics.
item.author: Jesse I

item.title: Featuring guest presenter Lady Love Potion
item.author: Jesse I

item.title: fruits ripe
item.description: Paying respects to the late Jamaican producer and singer Phil Pratt (born George Phillips) who passed away on February 25, with a full hour of his music... plus the beautiful new Fruits Ripe riddim from Zion I Kings, message music from the Virgin Islands, and contemporary roots reggae from Max Romeo's kids Xana and Azizzi.
item.author: Jesse I

item.title: why war
item.description: Featuring new music from Perfect Giddimani, Chezidek, Etana, Mista Savona & Mia Bloom, Joe Yorke, Marcus I, Dexta Daps, Micah Shemaiah, Vanzo, Queen Omega and nuff more... riddim juggling on the biggest riddim in dancehall right now, the WYFL riddim from DJ Mac & CrashDummy, as well as the Recovery riddim from Head Concussion... and some classic early 80s anti-war tunes.
item.author: Jesse I

item.title: late night blues
item.description: Featuring new music from Kabaka Pyramid, Protoje, Shenseea, Vybz Kartel, Buju Banton, Patoranking, Busy Signal, Capleton, Bushman, Dalwayne, YT, Marcus I and more... revisiting Stone Love's classic version of the "Give Me The Right" riddim... vintage 70s deejay tunes... and some sweet 70s roots singers.
item.author: Jesse I
